title: pinus nigra subsp. clusiana
intro: Pinus nigra subsp. clusiana, also known as the Austrian Black Pine or Cilician Pine, is a subspecies of evergreen conifer native to the mountainous regions of southern Europe and western Asia. The plant's bark, leaves, and seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds. The health benefits of Pinus nigra subsp. clusiana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steaming the leaves and young shoots, or by extracting the essential oils from the resin.
